Methods

ItemsRepeater(int, Func<int, Element>, in RepeatLayout, Func<int, string>?, int, Action<int>?, Action<int>?, Action<int, int>?, Action<int, int>?, ItemCollectionTransition?)

A data-driven items panel (WinUI's ItemsRepeater): count items × a template over a pluggable layout. Virtualizing layouts (Stack/Grid/Custom/Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid) realize only the window and recycle over the slab free-list — 0-alloc steady scroll; non-virtual layouts (Wrap/Inline) build the full child list (use for small collections). No built-in selection/focus — compose that above it (ItemsView is exactly that composition). E11-L2 surface: elementPrepared/elementClearing/elementIndexChanged are WinUI's ItemsRepeater lifecycle events (ItemsRepeater.idl:186-188 ElementPrepared/ElementClearing/ ElementIndexChanged), fired with item indices at realize time; visibleRange is the prefetch hook (first,last); transition maps ItemCollectionTransition onto the FLIP/Animate pipeline.

public static Element ItemsRepeater(int count, Func<int, Element> template, in RepeatLayout layout, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 4, Action<int>? elementPrepared = null, Action<int>? elementClearing = null, Action<int, int>? elementIndexChanged = null, Action<int, int>? visibleRange = null, ItemCollectionTransition? transition = null)

Parameters

count int
template Func<int, Element>
layout RepeatLayout
keyOf Func<int, string>
overscan int
elementPrepared Action<int>
elementClearing Action<int>
elementIndexChanged Action<int, int>
visibleRange Action<int, int>
transition ItemCollectionTransition?

Returns

Element

ItemsRepeater<T>(IReadOnlyList<T>, Func<int, T, Element>, in RepeatLayout, Func<int, string>?, int, Action<int>?, Action<int>?, Action<int, int>?, Action<int, int>?, ItemCollectionTransition?)

TYPED data binding (E11-L2): the WinUI ItemsSource + ItemTemplate pair as a generic overload — the template receives (index, item) with no cast and no per-item boxing. Count and item identity come from items; re-render with a new list (or wrap in a signal read) to update.

public static Element ItemsRepeater<T>(IReadOnlyList<T> items, Func<int, T, Element> template, in RepeatLayout layout, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 4, Action<int>? elementPrepared = null, Action<int>? elementClearing = null, Action<int, int>? elementIndexChanged = null, Action<int, int>? visibleRange = null, ItemCollectionTransition? transition = null)

Parameters

items IReadOnlyList<T>
template Func<int, T, Element>
layout RepeatLayout
keyOf Func<int, string>
overscan int
elementPrepared Action<int>
elementClearing Action<int>
elementIndexChanged Action<int, int>
visibleRange Action<int, int>
transition ItemCollectionTransition?

Returns

Element

Type Parameters

T
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
